phenomena.name: Schwa insertion
phenomena.description: Insertion of a weak vowel (schwa) inside or between consonant clusters.
practiceDrills: cluster-reduction: slow repetition of clusters without inserting vowels
minimal-contrast: practice minimal pairs with and without schwa insertions

phenomena.name: Consonant cluster simplification
phenomena.description: Simplifying consonant clusters by inserting vowels or dropping consonants (e.g., "school" → "iskool").
practiceDrills: cluster_slow_repeat: practice clusters slowly then accelerate
isolation-drill: isolate cluster consonants before merging into word

phenomena.name: Aspiration mismatch
phenomena.description: Weak or missing aspiration on voiceless plosives (p,t,k) compared to expected native-like burst.
practiceDrills: aspiration-puff: hold out a small puff after voiceless stops
minimal-voicing: contrast voiced vs voiceless pairs

phenomena.name: Final consonant reduction
phenomena.description: Dropping or weakening of final consonants, leading to missing endings in past tense or plurals.
practiceDrills: hold-final: sustain final consonant closure then release
word-chains: practice lists emphasizing final consonants

phenomena.name: Dentalization
phenomena.description: Tongue tip contacts the teeth for alveolar sounds, producing a dentalized quality.
practiceDrills: tip-placement: practice placing tongue behind upper alveolar ridge
minimal-contrast: practice between dentalized and non-dentalized targets

phenomena.name: Vowel neutralization
phenomena.description: Reduction of vowel quality towards a centralized schwa-like sound in unstressed syllables or even in stressed positions.
practiceDrills: vowel-targets: exaggerate full vowel quality in slow motion
contrast-drill: minimal pairs focusing on vowel distinction

phenomena.name: Stress shift
phenomena.description: Shifting primary lexical stress causing syllable timing and intelligibility changes.
practiceDrills: stress-tap: clap on stressed syllable and speak rhythm
metronome-drill: read sentences aligned to a metronome

phenomena.name: Syllable clipping
phenomena.description: Shortening or omitting weak syllables leading to compressed words.
practiceDrills: lengthen-syllable: hold vowel length for target syllables
syllable-count: chant syllable counts before saying the word

phenomena.name: Epenthetic vowel insertion
phenomena.description: Adding an extra vowel inside consonant clusters (e.g., 'film' → 'fi-lum').
practiceDrills: cluster-compress: say cluster repeatedly without vowels
tape-mimic: listen and mimic native cluster tokens

phenomena.name: Final consonant devoicing
phenomena.description: Voiced consonants at the ends of words become voiceless (e.g., 'bag' → 'back' quality).
practiceDrills: voicing-contrast: alternate voiced/voiceless word pairs
sustain-voiced: hold voicing through closure

phenomena.name: Glottal stop substitution
phenomena.description: Replacing plosive release with a glottal stop, affecting clarity of consonant release.
practiceDrills: release-practice: practice full oral release instead of glottal stop
contrast-words: target words with clear oral plosive release
